items.summary: In AppContext.tsx, implement background music using a module-level Audio variable (outside the component) so it is never recreated on re-renders or context re-mounts. On first provider mount, if the module-level audio variable is null: create new Audio('/assets/music/background.mp3'), set .loop = true, set .volume = 1.0, assign it to the module-level variable, attach an 'error' event listener that sets isMusicPlaying to false silently. Do NOT attempt autoplay — music starts paused. Expose isMusicPlaying (boolean, default false) and toggleMusic (calls audio.play() or audio.pause() based on current state, updates isMusicPlaying accordingly) via context. Wrap every single audio operation in try/catch that fails silently. The app must never crash regardless of whether the file exists or playback is blocked.

In AppShell.tsx, add a music toggle button that: (1) reads isMusicPlaying from AppContext via useContext, (2) calls toggleMusic from AppContext on click, (3) displays '♪' (filled music note) when isMusicPlaying is true and '🔇' (muted symbol) when isMusicPlaying is false, (4) applies a subtle CSS pink glow pulse animation (box-shadow pulsing from rgba(244,114,182,0.6) to transparent) only when isMusicPlaying is true, (5) if AppContext values are undefined, the button no-ops on click without crashing. Position the button in the top-right corner alongside the existing dark mode toggle.

Place a placeholder audio file note in the codebase: create the directory frontend/public/assets/music/ and add a README.md inside it instructing: 'Place your background music file here as background.mp3. This file is loaded by AppContext.tsx at /assets/music/background.mp3.' This ensures the directory exists in the build output and the path resolves correctly in ICP deployment.
